skill.name: uml-modeling
skill.description: Design, critique, and revise UML diagrams with a focus on correct modeling, clear boundaries, and readable notation.
skill.summary: Create and improve UML diagrams that communicate software structure, behavior, interactions, and deployment clearly.
skill.author: Carbocode

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but reviewed evidence shows Markdown UML guidance and inline diagram labels, not executable code. No scripts, runtime files, network calls, environment access, shell execution, credential handling, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed skill context. The skill is documentation-only and safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline UML Labels Flagged As External Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged lines are Markdown inline examples, file names, or UML transition labels. They do not execute shell commands or instruct the assistant to run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose or inline-code examples inside Markdown reference files. No executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: UML Terminology Flagged As Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged high-severity patterns occur in descriptions of UML classes, states, deployment artifacts, and modeling rules. They are not cryptographic APIs or algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled flagged locations contain documentation about UML modeling concepts. I found no hashing, encryption, certificate, or password-processing implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Modeling Questions Flagged As Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged reconnaissance lines ask diagram-selection and boundary questions for UML modeling. They do not collect host, network, account, or environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text is static UML guidance and does not request execution of discovery commands. No semantic intent to enumerate systems or networks was found.

content.value_statement: Teams often produce UML diagrams that mix goals, states, flows, and implementation details. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward correct diagram types and clearer models.
actual_capabilities: Chooses an appropriate UML diagram type from the user goal.
Creates self-contained UML models with explicit scope and boundaries.
Reviews diagrams for semantic errors and abstraction-level mismatches.
Separates structure, behavior, interaction, deployment, and use-case concerns.
Guides revisions for class, activity, state machine, sequence, and deployment diagrams.
Uses bundled reference files as the primary modeling knowledge base.
limitations: Does not render images by itself unless the host tool provides diagram rendering.
Requires enough domain context to name actors, boundaries, states, and responsibilities.
Does not verify generated diagrams against a live UML parser or renderer.
Focuses on UML modeling quality, not general graphic design or decorative visuals.

use_cases.title: Model A New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a feature description into the right UML diagram with clear scope, responsibilities, and relationships.

use_cases.title: Review Existing Diagrams
use_cases.description: Identify semantic issues, unclear labels, mixed abstraction levels, and missing context before publishing documentation.

use_cases.title: Clarify System Behavior
use_cases.description: Choose between activity, state machine, and sequence diagrams when behavior is hard to explain in prose.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic UML Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a known subject
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a UML diagram for [system or feature]. Show the main actors, components, states, or steps needed to explain [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Choose The Right Diagram Type
prompt_templates.scenario: User knows the modeling goal but not the notation
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a diagram that explains [question]. Choose the best UML diagram type, explain the choice briefly, then draft the diagram.

prompt_templates.title: Review And Repair A Diagram
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this UML diagram for semantic correctness, scope, abstraction level, and readability. Then provide a corrected version and explain key fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Split A Complex Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this system description and decide whether it needs multiple UML diagrams. Create the smallest useful set and explain what each diagram answers.

output_examples.input: Explain order checkout behavior as UML.
output_examples.output: A selected activity diagram with user, application, payment provider, and inventory responsibilities, plus guarded branches for payment failure and stock changes.

output_examples.input: Review my state diagram for account activation.
output_examples.output: A critique that separates stable account states from actions, renames transition effects, and adds missing events and guards.

output_examples.input: Show how services communicate during login.
output_examples.output: A sequence diagram plan with lifelines for user, client app, identity provider, API service, and data store.

best_practices: State the modeled subject and audience before asking for a diagram.
Use one diagram type for one main modeling question.
Ask for review when a diagram mixes workflow, lifecycle, structure, and deployment details.
anti_patterns: Requesting one diagram to show every class, service, state, and workflow.
Using a state machine for a procedure made of short-lived actions.
Copying source-code names directly when the audience needs conceptual behavior.

faq.question: Can this skill choose the UML diagram type for me?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to infer the best UML form when the user gives a modeling goal.

faq.question: Does it generate PlantUML or Mermaid?
faq.answer: It can guide textual diagram output when requested, but its main value is modeling correctness before notation.

faq.question: Can it review diagrams I already have?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks semantics, boundaries, abstraction level, self-containment, and readability.

faq.question: Which UML types does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers structure, behavior, interaction, deployment, use case, state machine, activity, sequence, and related UML diagrams.

faq.question: Is this only for software architects?
faq.answer: No. Engineers, technical writers, analysts, testers, and product teams can use it for clearer system explanations.

faq.question: Does it execute code or inspect systems?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains Markdown guidance and references for UML modeling. It does not include executable scripts.
